meta.title: AI Weekly Intel
meta.subtitle: An internal intelligence view of the most important OpenAI product and capability updates for business teams.

hero.headline: This week was about workflow upgrades, not just model upgrades.
hero.subhead: OpenAI paired a flagship model release with Excel integration, ChatGPT rollout changes, and broader Codex desktop availability. The common thread is lower friction between AI capability and actual office work.

summary_cards.label: Validated updates
summary_cards.detail: Five official, in-window product or capability updates passed the source and scope filter.

summary_cards.label: Source types
summary_cards.detail: The brief pulls from official OpenAI announcements and Help Center release notes rather than third-party summaries.

summary_cards.label: Dominant theme
summary_cards.detail: The strongest story is that OpenAI moved more value into familiar work surfaces such as ChatGPT, Excel, and enterprise desktops.

highlight_cards.title: GPT-5.4
highlight_cards.blurb: A flagship release that improves the ceiling for long-context, coding, and agentic business workflows.

highlight_cards.title: Excel integration
highlight_cards.blurb: The spreadsheet-heavy core of office work now has a more direct path to AI-assisted analysis.

highlight_cards.title: Windows Codex
highlight_cards.blurb: Enterprise deployment friction drops when the coding workflow reaches the desktop platform most companies already standardize on.

use_cases.title: Executive memo drafting
use_cases.detail: Use the flagship model for board notes, strategic options, and customer-facing summaries where narrative quality and long-context reasoning matter.

use_cases.title: Workbook review and financial analysis
use_cases.detail: Shorten the loop between workbook review and decision support by moving from spreadsheet data to narrative commentary inside the same workflow.

use_cases.title: Windows-based Codex pilot
use_cases.detail: Run an internal coding or automation pilot on the operating system most enterprise technical teams already use.

use_cases.title: Long-form internal drafting at speed
use_cases.detail: Use the larger output ceiling for faster first drafts of policy summaries, operating notes, and structured internal documents.

methodology.headline: Official-source, trailing-7-day selection
methodology.body: This briefing retains only official OpenAI product and capability updates published or materially updated between March 2 and March 8, 2026 inclusive. Supporting documents and policy-only items were logged but excluded from the main set.

cta.headline: Move from generic experimentation to focused deployment.
cta.body: The strongest next step is a compact pilot portfolio: one spreadsheet workflow, one long-form drafting workflow, and one internal tools or automation workflow.
